methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Manifold Bio is a Series A biotech company founded by Gleb Kuznetsov and Pierce Ogden that has raised $40M to date. The valuation analysis employs two triangulation methods against a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public companies in Biotech & Health.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, using the $40M total raised as a proxy base value. This method yields an implied valuation of $80M. Peer-median analysis identifies 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B Series B), Xaira Therapeutics ($2.7B Seed), and Neko Health ($1.8B Series B), with a median valuation of $1.3B and a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring using a 6.1x median P/S multiple across three biotech tickers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) was not applied due to absence of target revenue data.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $68M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $690M. This wide dispersion reflects substantial uncertainty around stage-appropriate valuation and peer selection. The stage-progression method anchors to observed capital deployment, while peer-median is driven by outsized valuations in the frontier-biotech space. Key uncertainties include: whether Manifold's technology trajectory justifies premium peer multiples, capital efficiency relative to comparables, and the likely dilution at next financing.
